sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO

performanceNotes: Shadow Cacturne is extremely glassy — requires shield or health advantage to realize its potential
Double grass core is vulnerable to fire and Incinerate users like Gourgeist
Lanturn handles threats the double grass line is weak to
description: Subscriber team from BJ 2018. Shadow Cacturne as a glassy attack-heavy lead with two bulky Pokemon covering each other in the back. First set ran Water Gun Lanturn, then switched to Spark for the rest.

performanceNotes: Amoonguss + Lanturn core is very strong in Spring Cup meta
Decidueye is glassy and struggles in 2-shield scenarios vs Empoleon
Team is alignment-dependent in the back; creator found it decent but not 90% win rate

sources.videoTitle: I used a Subscribers 90% WIN RATE TEAM  | Spring Cup Team | Pokémon GO Battle League
sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO

description: Subscriber's 90% win rate team. Decidueye lead forces alignment, Amoonguss is a neutral safe swap, Lanturn closes with bulk and shield advantage.

Three safe Spring Cup picks: Amoonguss lead with bulky coverage, Shadow Empoleon for closing power (drill peck KOs Tentacruel/Jellicent in 2 vs 3 for non-shadow), and Tentacruel whose water/poison typing neutralizes grass, resists water, and walls incinerate users.
performanceNotes: Weak to electric but otherwise very safe core
Shadow Empoleon chosen specifically for 2-drill-peck KO threshold on Tentacruel and Jellicent
Went positive overall but creator got bored of the meta

Lanturn lead as a core breaker walling Jellicent and Empoleon, with Amoonguss safe swap covering grass and water matchups, and Pelipper as closer spamming Weather Balls against steels and Hurricane against grasses.
